mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, it signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong real GDP growth and subdued inflation compresses the equity risk premium by confirming a soft-landing scenario, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5% while CPIAUCSL annualized inflation remains below 2.5%
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not outperform the unconditional mean by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 1.0% indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate throughput, which translates into higher equity valuations over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered episodes across a 10-year sample, indicating the signal captures noise rather than fundamental expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures directly reflect strengthening consumer demand, which improves near-term earnings guidance and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week without requiring regime filters.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 in all calendar periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high policy rates and elevated inflation trigger a compression in equity multiples as discount rates rise and real cash flows shrink, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and CPIAUCSL annualized inflation exceeds 3.0%
failure_condition: The dual-threshold signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.02 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market healing, which sustains consumer spending and lifts equity valuations over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200 thousand while UNRATE declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a positive predictive Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units signal robust construction activity and rising household formation, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter through wealth and investment channels.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances across a 10-year backtest, indicating the housing signal has lost its historical predictive power for equities.
